summary: Reintroduce custom property support to new uri parser
description: The old uri parser has a notion of validating known properties, for example [authMechanismProperties|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/master/lib/url_parser.js#L498-L518]. This needs to be ported to the new URI parser

summary: Replicaset tests for core use Server and require primary on port 31000
description: Many of the mongodb-core tests for replicasets use a {{Server}} instance and directly connect to what is generally the primary (port 31000). If this ever changes then things break dramatically. We should convert all of these tests to use {{configuration.newTopology}}.

summary: New URL parser rejects db names made only of numbers
description:
Originally reported here: https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/COMPASS-3090

 

Was able to recreate by connecting to the node driver directly and trying to authenticate with a db name of "001". Authentication will fail if \{useNewUrlParser: true} is passed, otherwise no error.

summary: Fix auth workflow with regards to auth mechanism resolution
description:
See [this comment | https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/pull/348#discussion_r217726742].

* If you do not provide a mechanism, or if you say "default" we need do to mechanism negotiation. As of MongoDB 4.0, this requires an ismaster to get saslSupportedMechs
* For mechanism negotiation to work, we need to have credentials available at the time of topology connect, which happens before the actual auth call. This way, we can add the saslSupportedMechs field to the first outgoing ismaster
* At the same time, there is legacy support for authenticating on connect, where you pass credentials in to the initial connect call, and immediately auth. This is a problem, since the credentials have not been mechanism negotiated yet, so they will still say default.

Lets find a way to do this that is neater

summary: SCRAM-SHA-256 tests are not actually run against replicaset
description: Our current {{SCRAM-SHA-256}} tests are not actually run against a replica set even when the environment is specified, because the URL omits a {{replicaSet}} option. Instead, the tests are run in single mode against one of the nodes.

summary: Add dynamicDefaults/overrides to options validator
description: Some options, like {{readPreference}}, have default values that are more complicated than others, like {{capped}}. We should add a dynamicDefaults/overrides object as an argument to the options validator. This will allow us to properly set defaults with complex logic or inheritance hierarchies.

summary: GridFSBucket upload stream throw deprecation warning
description: DeprecationWarning: collection.insert is deprecated. Use insertOne, insertMany or bulkWrite instead.

summary: running mongodb-test-runner -e sharded does not tear down sharded clusters
Implement assertArity function
description: We need an {{assertArity}} function for options validation without the OperationBuilder. This function will check that operations are called with the correct number of arguments.

description:
(node:29079) DeprecationWarning: collection.insert is deprecated. Use insertOne, insertMany or bulkWrite instead.
 at writeRemnant (/run/media/sergio/RAZIEL/LynxChan/src/be/node_modules/mongodb/lib/gridfs-stream/upload.js:517:16)
 at /run/media/sergio/RAZIEL/LynxChan/src/be/node_modules/mongodb/lib/gridfs-stream/upload.js:165:7

summary: Implement auth connection string tests
Port authentication support to new Topology class
description: The new unified {{Topology}} class lacks support for authentication, it should be ported from the existing topologies.

summary: Update atlas connectivity tests to use new auth credentials
description: Aut has been fully refactored for both native and core drivers, the old Atlas connectivity tests will no longer work as written and need to be updated.

summary: Setting useNewUrlParser: true in connection causes issue.
Driver overwrites database from uri with authSource from options
description:
Configuration:
* user execute query in "DB_query"
* user authorization stored in "DB_auth" (user has no acces to that db)
* config:
  url: mongodb://user:password@host:port/DB_query
  options: {"authSource": "DB_auth"}

const client = await MongoClient.connect(url, options);
const result = await client.db().collection(collection).find(query, fields);

Expected: query executed in "DB_query"
Actual: query executed in "DB_auth"

This thing seems caused by [this change|https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/commit/923ceb0176341370fdda573ffb6d11024d1dc81e] i get error "MongoError: not authorized on DB_auth to execute command".
Db in url is differ than than authSource, after latest upgrade driver try to execute query in authSource instead of db specified in url.

Behavior exists in 3.x and 4.x mongodb, standalone and replica set

summary: unify collation support in topology
docs tutorial shows connecting with MongoClient() incorrectly
Test deleteMany and updateMany with retryWrites=true
description: See DRIVERS-574 for details.

summary: Document properties of KerberosServer and KerberosClient
description: The newly introduced {{KerberosServer}} and {{KerberosClient}} classes in the kerberos module have documentation for attached methods, but not the properties associated with them. These should be documented.

summary: node driver api createIndex() can't capture specific error
kerberos module makes incorrect null checks for user information
Kerberos Authentication on Windows should not require password
description:
Hi,

*Current behavior*
The driver requires the username (user principal name) and password when using the GSSAPI authentication on Windows. 

*Expected behavior*
Only the UPN should be required for Kerberos auth on Windows.

Thanks,

Davenson Lombard

summary: Code of ensureIndex and createIndex are inconsistent, yet the docs state they should be an alias of one another
description:
The [documentation|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/method/db.collection.ensureIndex/] of MongoDB states that `ensureIndex` should be deprecated, and an alias of `createIndex`:

{quote}db.collection.ensureIndex(keys, options)
Deprecated since version 3.0.0: db.collection.ensureIndex() is now an alias for db.collection.createIndex().
{quote}

However, upon analysing the code, they seem to have clearly different functionality and `ensureIndex` does not in fact look like a simple alias for `createIndex`:

*createIndex*
https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.0.0/lib/db.js#L1029

*ensureIndex*
https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.0.0/lib/db.js#L1113

Expected behaviour is that in light of the deprecation, `ensureIndex` should simply call `createIndex`.

Currently there seem to be some users who still rely on the side effects present in `ensureIndex`, but they should probably receive a deprecation notice as well as instructions on how to migrate to using `createIndex` instead.

summary: Fix sporadic build failures in mongodb-core
Remove circular references to types in js-bson
description: Each type defined in {{js-bson}} has a [circular reference to itself|https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/blob/master/lib/bson/code.js#L25-L26]. We should remove these in the next major version of the module.

summary: Fully validate options for all operations
description: There are likely operations for which all options are not currently documented throughout the driver. We need to fully trace options for every operation and ensure all schemas are correct.

summary: js-bson should allow 0xFFFD to be encoded/decoded
description:
https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/issues/277

See: https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/blob/v3.0.2/lib/bson/parser/deserializer.js#L130. When we deserialize BSON, we take any occurrence of 0xFFFD to be a serializer error, which does not account for the fact that a user may want to intentionally encode 0xFFFD as a character.

Options to resolve them:

1. Use https://www.npmjs.com/package/utf8
Pros: Will throw on failure to decode utf8
Cons: Seems to not use buffers?

2. Use https://www.npmjs.com/package/utf-8-validate
Pros: Same as above
Cons: Is C++

3. Buffer.from(input.toString()).compare(input) === 0
Pros: Barely need to do anything
Cons: Needs alloc of new Buffer. Could be bad for large strings
Cons: Can we use Buffer.compare at specific index positions?
Cons: Will this work in the browser?

4. Write our own utf8 validator
Pros: It will be tailored to our needs
Cons: Time, possibility for errors

summary: CI Test against Node 10.x (Dubnium)
description:
Node 10.x (dubnium) entered Active LTS on October 31st, 2018. We should start testing against it in all of our CI environments.

 
 * -Travis node-mongodb-native- Already Done
 * -Evergreen node-mongodb-native- [https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/pull/1860]
 * -Travis mongodb-core-: [https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/pull/371]
 * -Evergreen mongodb-core-: [https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/pull/371]
 * -Travis js-bson- Already done
 * Travis bson-ext <-- Not sure if this is necessary.
 * -Travis mongodb-extjson- Unnecessary; library getting merged into js-bson

summary: BSON ObjectID constructor should throw if passed-in string uses characters greater than 8 bits.
description:
Ported from [https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/issues/281]

 
{quote}There have been some similar issues open for this before ([#106|https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/issues/106], [#112|https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/issues/112]), but I don't think they made the right point.
 The issue happens indeed because the argument is not validated.

Providing {{new ObjectId('000000000000')}} gives the correct console.log of: {{303030303030303030303030}}.

Javascript strings are UTF-16 encoded. I create my ObjectId using:
 {{new ObjectId('ŽŽŽŽŽŽŽŽŽŽŽŽ')}}
 where Ž is this latin letter [http://www.ltg.ed.ac.uk/~richard/utf-8.cgi?input=%C5%BD&mode=char]

The construction doesn't fail, but while trying to console.log this ObjectId gives:
 {{undefinedundefinedundefinedundefinedundefinedundefinedundefinedundefinedundefinedundefinedundefinedundefined}}
{quote}
To fix this, we should throw if a length-12 string is passed in with characters with a charCode > 256

summary: Don't issue warning for missing kerberos module
description: The core driver attempts to optionally require kerberos and spits out a warning if it can't find it, see [here|https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/blob/master/lib/utils.js#L48]. This is annoying for users who aren't using kerberos, we should remove it.

summary: Explain useNewUrlParser
description: At [http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/3.1/api/MongoClient.html], please explain what *useNewUrlParser* does. There's [a lot of confusion on StackOverflow|https://stackoverflow.com/questions/50448272/avoid-current-url-string-parser-is-deprecated-warning-by-setting-usenewurlpars] about that.

summary: Integrate options validation into Admin, Collection, Cursor, Db, MongoClient
description: Once options validation and the OperationBuilder are implemented, we can integrate them into all operations.

summary: Extended JSON should default to using native number type in non-strict mode
description:
From [~fonger]'s pull request

bq. MongoDB node.js driver will store JavaScript Number as Double type by default. So I think parsing in non-strict mode, Double should cast to number. Furthermore, JavaScript numbers are always stored as double precision floating point numbers, following the IEEE 754 standard.

see [this pull request|https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-extjson/pull/21]

summary: Modernize and improve bundling of js-bson
description:
* Update all of js-bson to use ES6 notation where it makes sense
* Standardize what is exported from the module
* Integrate bundle changes from [~eric.daniels]
* Publish v4.0.0 of the module

summary: Update Travis version of MongoDB
description:
From [this PR|https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/pull/371]:

bq. we should bump the default tested version of mongo from 3.4 to something more reasonable

summary: Validate options in findAndModify and findAndRemove
description: {{findAndModify}} and {{findAndRemove}} are deprecated, but still used internally. Because of their internal use, we should add their options to options validation for any method that calls them.

summary: New url parser ignores auth provide via options
description: In my haste to pass auth spec tests, I ignored the fact that we support passing in auth information through options in {{MongoClient}}s constructor.

summary: Authentication fails when database is not specified in connection string
Need to double-URI-encode username/password with mongodb+srv:// protocol
Mongos topology leaks memory on server reconnect
Db should take in client instead of topology
description: If {{Db}} takes in {{client}} instead of {{topology}}, we can use the {{client}}'s {{optionsValidationLevel}} and replace every {{topology}} with {{client.topology}}.

summary: kerberos module does not honor optional nature of `defaultDomain` in `checkPassword`
description: See the following [github issue|https://github.com/mongodb-js/kerberos/issues/91] for more details.

summary: Latest BSON library unable to serialize ObjectID from previous versions
description: See [github issue|https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/issues/289]

summary: Evaluate and correct monitoring in new SDAM layer
description: The current rewrite of SDAM failed to

summary: GridFS Options Management
description: Add options validation for \{{GridFS}} operations.

summary: Simplify cursor `nextFunction` to combine cursor initialization and initial find command
description: The current implementation of core's {{nextFunction}} is incredibly complex, and makes a number of recursive calls needlessly. We should simplify this code path, primarily ensuring that cursor initialization also makes the first find command call. This will allows for the next step in the wire protocol refactor, which is to make {{WireProtocol.prototype.command}} actually take a callback, and provide a consistent API across wire protocol methods.

summary: Replace "shallowClone" util function with Object.assign calls
description: {{shallowClone(x)}} can be accomplished with native {{Object.assign({}, x)}}, and that is an understood idiom. We should get rid of the unnecessary util function in our code.

summary: Introduce wire protocol method `query`
description: The {{WireProtocol.prototype.command}} has a hack to introspect the incoming command, determine if it is a find command, and switch its internal logic if so. We shouldn't have to introspect here, we can simply call a different command when we are attempting a query. Additionally, this will allow us to defer checking whether commands are supported when executing a query, switching between a plain legacy {{OP_QUERY}} and a modern, "commandified" {{OP_QUERY}} against the {{$cmd}} collection.

summary: Modernize and clean up WireProtocol implementation
description: Convert the WireProtocol to a class, remove duplication and streamline options passing

summary: Refactor wire protocol's command method to take a callback
description:
The {{WireProtocol.prototype.command}} method is the current odd duck out in the methods provided on the wire protocol handler, in that it actually just _prepares_ a command, rather than executing it. This is done primarily for cursors, since they build the command and save it off internally. Refactoring this method to take a command accomplishes a few things:
 * Makes the API for this class consistent, allowing for easy refactoring of the actual code - or even allowing the code to be moves to another location (perhaps directly on {{Connection}}, so we can move server selection elsewhere
 * Allows locations in the code that are building their own {{Query}} instances to reuse the {{command}} method, so that we can reduce the number of places we have to make wire protocol based decisions (is collation supported?) 
 * Once all locations in the code are using this path, we can easily switch between OP_QUERY and OP_MSG, satisfying the full implementation of OP_MSG
